messages.content:
Identify one characteristic for each critical thinking approaches listed below. Briefly explain how each characteristic is used in each approach. 

Critical Thinking Approach	Characteristic	Explanation on How Each Characteristic is Used in Each Approach
a.	Analysis		   
b.	Synthesis	     	     
c.	Evaluation

a. Analysis - The characteristic of analysis is breaking down complex information into smaller parts to understand its structure and how it works. This approach is useful in critical thinking as it allows one to examine issues and arguments in detail, identifying the underlying assumptions and evidence used to support them.

b. Synthesis - Synthesis is the characteristic of combining different elements to create a new whole or solution. In critical thinking, it enables one to take various pieces of information or ideas from different sources and integrate them into a unique position or viewpoint.

c. Evaluation - The characteristic of evaluation is assessing the strengths and weaknesses of a particular argument or solution and forming a judgment based on evidence and criteria. In critical thinking, this approach allows one to evaluate the accuracy, credibility, relevance, and coherence of arguments and evidence, thus making informed decisions.

Briefly describe how each learning environment listed below may be used in an organisation. 

Learning Environment 	Description 
a.	Classroom Learning	     
b.	Distance Learning

a. Classroom Learning - Classroom learning is the traditional form of learning that takes place in a physical classroom setting, where learners are taught by a teacher or instructor. This environment is useful in an organisation for training new employees, providing ongoing professional development, and in-house training programmes for staff. Classroom learning allows for hands-on learning, group collaboration, and face-to-face interaction with instructors, which can be beneficial for engaging and motivating learners.

b. Distance Learning - Distance learning is an online or virtual learning environment where learners can access learning materials and interact with instructors and peers remotely. This environment is useful in an organisation for delivering training programmes to remote or geographically dispersed employees, facilitating ongoing professional development, and providing flexible learning opportunities. Distance learning allows learners to access learning materials at their own pace and convenience, interact with instructors and peers through online forums and discussions, and complete assessments and evaluations remotely.

Complete the table listed below.
▪	Identify two learning systems that can be used for the listed organisational learning environment. 
Learning systems are the different ways that learning can be delivered using each learning environment listed below. (e.g., In-House Training can use a classroom learning environment.)
▪	Briefly describe how each learning system is used in an organisation. 

Learning Environment	Learning System	Description of Use in an Organisation
a.	Classroom Learning	i.	     	     
	ii.	     	     
b.	Distance Learning	i.	     	     
	ii.

Learning Environment	Learning System	Description of Use in an Organisation
a. Classroom Learning	i. In-House Training	In-house training is delivered through classroom learning environments, where new employees or existing staff receive training on skills relevant to their job roles. This system fosters interactive learning, group collaboration, and allows learners to ask questions and receive immediate feedback.
	ii. Apprenticeships	Apprenticeships are a form of training that involves combining classroom learning with on-the-job training, where learners can acquire the necessary skills for their job role. This system is used to bridge the gap between theory and practice, giving learners the opportunity to apply what they've learned in a real-world setting.

b. Distance Learning	i. E-Learning	E-learning is a form of distance learning that uses digital technology, internet access, and digital platforms to make learning materials available online and facilitate remote communication between learners and instructors. This system can be used for in-house training, professional development, and ongoing skills enhancement, providing flexibility to learners who need to complete training at their own pace and convenience, regardless of location.
	ii. Virtual Instructor-Led Training (VILT)	VILT is a distance learning system that enables learners to access real-time training sessions via video conferencing technology, while allowing group participation and live interaction with instructors. VILT is used when learners are in different physical locations and require real-time training on specific skills or topics. It is useful for providing continuous training to employees, reducing travel expenses, and accommodating learners who work remotely.

Complete the table below:
▪	Identify two requirements for the legislation below that are related to Bounce Fitness’ Customer Relationship Management Policy and Procedures.
▪	Briefly explain how the identified requirements are related to the Bounce Fitness procedure.
Access the links below:
▪	Customer Relationship Management Policy and Procedures
https://bouncefitness.precisiongroup.com.au/policies-and-procedures/customer-relationship-management/
▪	Privacy Act 1988
https://www.legislation.gov.au/Series/C2004A03712
You must access the latest version of the Privacy Act 1988.

Legislation	Requirements	Explanation
Privacy Act 1988	i. The Australian Privacy Principles (APPs)	APPs require organisations to establish and maintain practices, procedures, and systems to ensure compliance with the principles, including the collection, storage, use, and disclosure of personal information. Bounce Fitness' Customer Relationship Management Policy and Procedures need to ensure compliance with APPs, specifically with regards to storage, accessing, and sharing personal information of customers and employees.
	ii. Breach Notification Obligations	The Privacy Act 1988 requires organisations to notify the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals if there has been an eligible data breach. Bounce Fitness needs to have procedures in place in case of a data breach to ensure timely notification and documentation of the breach and its potential impact, including steps taken to mitigate the breach's effects.

Complete the table below:
▪	Identify two requirements for the legislation below related to Bounce Fitness’s staff recruitment policy.
▪	Briefly explain how the identified requirements are related to the policy.
Access the links below to assist you in answering this question: 
▪	Staff Recruitment Policy 
https://bouncefitness.precisiongroup.com.au/policies-and-procedures/human-resource/.
▪	Equal Employment Opportunity (Commonwealth Authorities) Act 1987
https://www.legislation.gov.au/Series/C2004A03429
You must access the latest version of the Equal Employment Opportunity (Commonwealth Authorities) Act 1987.

Legislation	Requirements	Explanation
Equal Employment Opportunity (Commonwealth Authorities) Act 1987	i. Merit-based selection	Bounce Fitness's Staff Recruitment Policy needs to use merit-based selection criteria, such as qualifications, skills, knowledge, and experience, to ensure that all applicants can compete on an equal footing for job vacancies without discrimination or favouritism. The Act requires Commonwealth entities to employ staff based on their merit, regardless of sex, race, disability, age, or other personal attributes.
	ii. Non-discrimination	Bounce Fitness's Staff Recruitment Policy must state explicitly that the organisation does not discriminate against job applicants based on personal characteristics protected by law, such as sex, sexual orientation, race, age, religion, or political views. The Act requires Commonwealth entities to provide equal employment opportunity to all staff and job applicants and take affirmative action to eliminate discrimination, harassment, or victimisation in the workplace.

Question 15 
Complete the tables below:
a.	Critical Thinking 
▪	Briefly explain an action that you can do in the workplace that will help an employee develop the critical thinking skills listed below.
b.	Creative Thinking Skills 
▪	Briefly explain an action that you can do in the workplace that will help an employee develop the creative thinking skills listed below.

A.	Critical Thinking 
Critical Thinking Skill	Brief Explanation of an Action in the Workplace That Will Help Develop Each Critical Thinking Skill
a.	Research	     
b.	Avoid Assumptions	     
B.	Creative Thinking
Creative Thinking Skill	Brief Explanation of an Action in the Workplace That Will Help Develop Each Creative Thinking Skill
a.	Imagine Possibilities 	     
b.	Open-mindedness

A. Critical Thinking

Critical Thinking Skill	Brief Explanation of an Action in the Workplace That Will Help Develop Each Critical Thinking Skill
a. Research	Conducting research involves gathering and analysing relevant information from credible sources to make informed decisions. To develop this skill, employees can be encouraged to research a topic related to their job role before making important decisions or solve a problem.
b. Avoid Assumptions	Assumptions can limit creativity, hinder problem-solving, and create an unfair work environment. To develop this skill, employees can be taught to avoid making assumptions and seek factual evidence to support their arguments or ideas. Managers can also provide employees with scenarios where assumptions may lead to errors and ask them to critically assess the situation.

B. Creative Thinking

Creative Thinking Skill	Brief Explanation of an Action in the Workplace That Will Help Develop Each Creative Thinking Skill
a. Imagine Possibilities	Imagining possibilities involves brainstorming and thinking outside the box to come up with innovative solutions to problems or challenges. To develop this skill, employees can be encouraged to generate and share ideas with their team members, participate in brainstorming sessions, or attend training sessions on creative problem-solving.
b. Open-mindedness	Open-mindedness involves being receptive to new ideas and perspectives, which can help generate creative and innovative solutions. To develop this skill, employees can be encouraged to challenge their biases, listen to others' perspectives, and be open to constructive feedback. Managers can also promote open-mindedness by creating a safe work environment where employees feel comfortable sharing their ideas without fear of criticism or retribution.

Question 14 
Complete the tables below:
a.	Critical Thinking Skills
▪	Identify at least one team building activity that will promote the development of each critical thinking skill.  
▪	Briefly explain how each team building activity identified promotes each critical thinking skill 
b.	Creative Thinking Skills 
▪	Identify at least one team building activity that will promote the development of each creative thinking skill.  
▪	Briefly explain how each team building activity identified promotes each creative thinking skill

A.	Critical Thinking Skills
Critical Thinking Skills	Team Building Activity for Critical Thinking 	Brief Explanation of How Each Activity Promotes a Team’s Critical Thinking Skills
a.	Problem Solving Skill	     	     
b.	Consider Alternatives 	     	     

B.     Creative Thinking Skills
Creative Thinking Skills	Team Building Activity for Creative Thinking 	Brief Explanation of How Each Activity Promotes Each Creative Thinking Skill
a.	Thinking Outside the Box 	     	     
b.	Brainstorm for solutions

A. Critical Thinking Skills

Critical Thinking Skills	Team Building Activity for Critical Thinking	Brief Explanation of How Each Activity Promotes a Team’s Critical Thinking Skills
a. Problem-Solving Skills	Riddles and puzzles team building activities can help improve problem-solving skills in teams. These games encourage creative thinking, force employees to think outside the box, and develop their skills in finding solutions to complex problems.
b. Considering Alternatives	Critical incident scenarios can help teams consider alternatives by discussing hypothetical situations. By asking each team member to offer possible solutions, the group can discuss various options, leading to better decision-making and enhanced critical thinking skills.

B. Creative Thinking Skills

Creative Thinking Skills	Team Building Activity for Creative Thinking	Brief Explanation of How Each Activity Promotes Each Creative Thinking Skill
a. Thinking Outside the Box	An escape room activity is a great way to promote thinking outside the box by challenging teams to solve puzzles and find hidden clues. The game requires creativity, imaginative thinking, and teamwork to come up with innovative solutions to escape. This activity allows teams to think beyond the conventional methods and develop their creative thinking skills.
b. Brainstorming for Solutions	A group problem-solving activity allows teams to brainstorm solutions to complex problems, encourages open dialogue, and helps develop creative thinking skills. Team members can offer suggestions, evaluate, refine and choose the best solution. It allows teams to work collaboratively and role-play various scenarios, improving their problem-solving abilities.

Question 13
Complete the following table by identifying:
▪	Your current workplace industry.
If you currently do not have a workplace, use the industry you are familiar with.
▪	Identify the best practice used in the identified industry to instruct others on critical and creative thinking methods. 
Industry best practice for instructing others refers to the most effective method for teaching people in that industry. 
Responses here will be used to answer the following guide questions.
Current workplace industry	     
Industry best practice to instructing	     
Complete the table below by identifying the following:
a.	One workplace task in your industry that uses each method provided below.
b.	One way of applying your identified industry’s best practice to teach others on how to perform each identified workplace task. 
Type of Thinking	Method	Workplace Task	Application of Industry Best Practice to Teach the Workplace Task Using the Method	
a.	Critical Thinking	Scaffolding 	     	     
b.	Creative Thinking	Brainstorming

Current workplace industry: Healthcare

Industry best practice to instructing: Simulation-based learning

Type of Thinking	Method	Workplace Task	Application of Industry Best Practice to Teach the Workplace Task Using the Method	
a. Critical Thinking	Scaffolding	Taking patient histories	Simulation-based learning can be used to teach critical thinking in healthcare to novice learners, where they are gradually introduced to new concepts and techniques, learning at their own pace with guidance from an instructor. Learners start with simple cases, building their skills through step-by-step instructions and feedback, and eventually advance to more complex cases.
b. Creative Thinking	Brainstorming	Developing New Care Programs or Services	Brainstorming sessions can encourage the generation of new ideas, with participants encouraged to share their thoughts and build on others' contributions without judgment or criticism. Simulator-based learning can be used to teach how to develop new care programs or services in healthcare, guiding learners through various scenarios and asking critical questions that promote innovative thinking. The focus should be on ideation, creativity, and imagination, with learners encouraged to build on others' ideas and look for out-of-the-box solutions.

Question 11 
Access Bounce Fitness’ Market Research Procedure in the link below. Complete the table in the following page by: 
a.	Identify one source that can provide additional information related to the procedure. 
b.	Record the source of your information.
c.	Answer the questions that follow to assess the reliability of your source.
Market Research Procedure
Title:      
Source:      
Criteria	Explanation 
Accuracy	Does the source provide valid information for your research?     
Authority	Is the content authored and published by a reputable and qualified organisation or person?    
Audience	Who is the intended audience?     
Is the information appropriate for the intended audience?     
Relevance	Is the source relevant to your research topic?    
Likelihood of bias	Is the information provided in this source biased?     
Based on your evaluation, is this source reliable or not? Tick Yes or No.
√□	YES
□	NO
Explain why or why not:

Title: Market Research Procedure
Source: https://bouncefitness.precisiongroup.com.au/policies-and-procedures/marketing/market-research-procedure/

Criteria	Explanation 
Accuracy	The information provided in the source appears accurate, supported by current research data, and provides a clear outline of market research procedures.
Authority	The Bounce Fitness website is owned and managed by Precision Group, a leading provider of integrated services, including marketing and communications. The information provided on the website is authored by qualified professionals and reviewed by relevant stakeholders before publication, indicating high levels of authority and expertise.
Audience	The intended audience for the Market Research Procedure is Bounce Fitness employees involved in developing and implementing marketing strategies, as well as stakeholders who may be interested in the organisation's marketing practices. The information provided is appropriate for the intended audience, presented in an easy-to-understand format that covers all relevant aspects of market research.
Relevance	The Market Research Procedure is relevant to the research topic of market research procedures in the fitness industry, providing a clear and comprehensive overview of effective practices and strategies.
Likelihood of bias	The information provided appears unbiased, objective, and supported by research data. The procedure outlines industry-standard practices for market research, and there is no evidence of any vested interests or external influences that may have influenced the content.
Based on your evaluation, is this source reliable or not? Tick Yes or No.
√□	YES
□	NO
Explain why or why not: The Market Research Procedure provided by Bounce Fitness is a reliable source of information. The information is accurate, authoritative, relevant, unbiased, and targeted at the appropriate audience. The source is credible, and the procedures described align with industry standard practices for conducting market research.
